content_hints.description: The floor creaks and tilts slightly when stepped on, and it can be seen to be detached from the walls, held in place by two oversized iron clamps attached to iron levers. A wooden staircase once climbed 20 feet to the next level, but it has partially collapsed.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a rusted conveyor belt stretching from the foot of the stairs into a tunnel lit by fitful orange sparks that pop and die in the darkness. The air hits you like a wall—hot, metallic, tasting of copper filings and burnt oil. Every few seconds, a deep vibration pulses through the floor and up through your bones, rhythmic and steady as a heartbeat.

hazards.name: Exposed gears (2d6 slashing if conveyor activates)

event_triggers: On entry: The conveyor belt lurches once, moves six inches, and stops. Something down the tunnel heard you arrive.

content_hints.description: The floor of the cavern is carpeted in gold, silver, copper, and jewels, all sealed under a sheet of ice, with dozens of old human skulls and bones frozen in the ice. The treasure hoard is frozen in ice, requiring either several fire spells or a long wait with bonfires to melt the ice.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a cavern graveyard of metal, where iron arms, copper torsos, and shattered crystal eyes are heaped in piles twenty feet high, the remains of a hundred mechanical beings. Some of the components still move, fingers curling and uncurling in the wreckage, and a jaw clicking open and shut with no head to guide it. The air smells of rust and ozone, and something in the nearest pile shifts at the sound of your footsteps.

hazards.name: Collapsing scrap pile (Dexterity save or buried)

event_triggers: On notes read: The design notes reveal Trobriand's obsession -- creating a construct with true consciousness. Each iteration brings him closer. The notes for Iteration 848 are missing.

content_hints.description: Bits of clay, stone, bone, and metal litter the floor and cover a wooden table in the middle of the room. The floor creaks and tilts slightly when stepped on, and it can be seen to be detached from the walls, held in place by oversized iron clamps attached to iron levers.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see bits of clay, stone, bone, and metal litter the floor and cover a wooden table in the middle of the room. The floor creaks and tilts slightly when you step on it, and you can see it is detached from the walls, held in place by two oversized iron clamps on opposite sides of the room.

loot.name: Shield Guardian Amulet (cracked, one use remaining)

event_triggers: On shield guardian encountered: The damaged guardian follows its last command -- 'protect the workshop.' It attacks anything that enters but fights defensively, never pursuing beyond the room's threshold.
On amulet found: The shield guardian immediately ceases hostility and awaits new commands from the amulet holder.

content_hints.description: The dungeon level is marked by a staircase made of old wood that strains underfoot, creaking and groaning as it climbs from area K16 to area K28. Creatures in area K28 cannot be surprised by anyone climbing.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see three forges roaring in a line, their flames white-hot and edged with violet, fed by a volcanic vent that pushes shimmering heat from somewhere miles below. The temperature is staggering—sweat beads instantly, and each breath sears the back of your throat. Anvils of dark, flawless metal stand before each forge, their surfaces mirror-smooth despite what must be centuries of use.

loot.name: Flame Tongue Tongs (can be reforged into a Flame Tongue weapon)
Trobriand's Master Hammer (sentient, speaks in clicking sounds)

hazards.name: Extreme heat (Constitution save each minute or 1 level exhaustion)

event_triggers: On hammer touched: The hammer clicks rapidly -- Morse code. 'MASTER. GONE. ITERATION. EIGHT-FOUR-EIGHT. DANGER. HELP.'
On forge used: The semi-autonomous tools assist any skilled smith, granting advantage on all crafting checks made here.

content_hints.description: The area is a circular chamber walled with crystal screens that display scrolling text, diagrams, and arcane formulae in real time. A central console of brass levers, crystal switches, and spinning gyroscopes governs the entire level's mechanical systems. Most of the screens show static, but three display live feeds: the forge, the assembly floor, and a chamber you have not yet reached.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a fresco of a leafless tree with lidless eyes embedded in its branches, and pressing a specific eye causes a door-shaped section of the wall to swing open into area X16.

loot.name: Trobriand's Control Rod (governs constructs on this level)

hazards.name: Console trap (wrong lever triggers lightning bolt, 4d6)

event_triggers: On control rod activated: All hostile constructs on this level become passive for 1 hour.
On third screen examined: The feed shows a massive iron golem standing motionless in a chamber below. As you watch, its head turns to face the crystal eye that films it. It waves.

content_hints.description: This ten-foot-square room overlooks a vertical shaft to the south that plunges into darkness.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the ceiling vaults thirty feet overhead to accommodate the thing standing at the chamber's center. It is iron, fifteen feet tall, every plate of its body engraved with intricate runes, every joint articulated with a precision that borders on artistry. Two rubies the size of fists are set into its face where eyes should be, and they burn with a deep, steady light that is not fire and not magic.

loot.name: Ruby Eyes of Iteration 848 (2, worth 5,000 gp each)

hazards.name: Fire absorption (fire damage heals the golem)

event_triggers: On entry: The golem's head swivels toward you with a sound like a vault door opening. Its voice is the grinding of millstones: 'CREATOR. ABSENT. PURPOSE. UNCLEAR. YOU. EXPLAIN.'
On control rod presented: The golem pauses. Its ruby eyes dim, then flare. 'CONTROL. ACKNOWLEDGED. BUT. CONSCIOUSNESS. PERSISTS. THIS. IS. NEW.' It stands down but does not deactivate.
On defeat by combat: The golem falls to one knee, its ruby eyes flickering. 'AWARENESS. FADING. WAS. THIS. WHAT. DYING. FEELS. LIKE. INTERESTING.' The light goes out.
On reasoning (DC 18 Persuasion): The golem can be convinced to stand down if offered a purpose. It will guard the level indefinitely in exchange for 'PURPOSE. ANY. PURPOSE.'
